steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Identify the circle's center and radius
steps.content: Compare $(x+3)^2 + (y-18)^2 = 169$ to the standard form $(x-h)^2 + (y-k)^2 = r^2$. Center: $(-3,\ 18)$. Radius: $\sqrt{169} = 13$.

steps.title: Find the range of valid $x$-values on the circle
steps.content: The $x$-coordinate of any point on the circle lies within one radius of the center's $x$-coordinate: $$-3 - 13 \le a \le -3 + 13$$ $$-16 \le a \le 10$$

steps.title: Test each choice against the range
steps.content:
• A) $a = -17$: $-17 \lt -16$, outside the range — eliminate.
  • B) $a = -11$: $-16 \le -11 \le 10$, inside the range — valid.
  • C) $a = 13$: $13 \gt 10$, outside the range — eliminate.
  • D) $a = 18$: $18 \gt 10$, outside the range — eliminate.
That matches Choice B — -11.
